title: dalea subvillosa
intro: Dalea subvillosa, also known as the hairy prairie clover or hairy dalea, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dalea subvillosa include its ability to support digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Dalea subvillosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
